process_name: B -> phi K_S penguin CP asymmetry
implementation_difficulty_reason: B033 needs a Delta B=1 nonleptonic b -> s sbar s operator and amplitude treatment. The existing Delta F=2 SLL/SLR/VLL/VRR/LR1/LR2 neutral-meson machinery does not compute B -> phi K decay amplitudes, strong phases, or time-dependent penguin CP likelihoods.

grep_commands: rg -n "phi.?K|phiK|B.?->.?phi|B.*phi|beta_eff|sin2.?beta|S_CP|C_CP" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "b\s*->\s*s|q\s*(q-bar|qbar|\\bar\{q\})\s*s|penguin|charmless|time-dependent CP|B0.*phi|phi.*K" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'

rg -n "DEFAULT_DELTA_F2_INPUTS|evaluate_bd_mixing|evaluate_bs_mixing|MODERN_PHENOMENOLOGY_SYSTEM_IDS|B_d neutral|B_s neutral" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g '!*.ipynb'
evidence: Focused B033 greps found no direct implementation of B -> phi K, beta_eff, sin2beta_eff, b -> s sbar s penguin CP asymmetries, or time-dependent charmless B CP observables in the requested implementation/test directories.
Nearest adjacent code only: quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:209 defines the Delta F=2 input registry.
Nearest adjacent code only: quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:903 evaluates B_d mixing.
Nearest adjacent code only: quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:922 evaluates B_s mixing.
Nearest adjacent code only: quarkConstraints/modern/phenomenology.py:23 lists epsilon_K, K, B_d, B_s, and D0 as modern phenomenology systems; it does not include Delta B=1 penguin decays.

PKA draft, source snapshots, and code-coverage grep completed.

CA batch ca_w23_beauty verified CHK-1 through CHK-8.

status_history.notes: All measured and quoted prediction value claims verified against fetched source pages or local source snapshots.

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(S_{\phi K_S}\) and \(C_{\phi K_S}\), equivalently
\(\sin(2\beta_{\rm eff})_{\phi K^0}\) and \(C_{\phi K^0}\) in the HFLAV
convention.
This entry covers the time-dependent CP asymmetry in the penguin-dominated
beauty decay \(B^0\to\phi K_S^0\), usually reported through
\(\sin(2\beta_{\rm eff})\) and \(C_{\phi K^0}\).  It probes the
\(b\to s\bar s s\) loop amplitude and is interpreted by comparing its weak
phase with the reference \(b\to c\bar c s\) value from
\(B^0\to J/\psi K_S^0\).
sections.pdg_prose:
The source-of-truth numerical block is \texttt{pdg\_or\_equivalent} in
\texttt{B033.yaml}.  HFLAV Summer 2025
(\texttt{hflav\_triangle\_summer2025\_phiK0}) gives, for the \(\phi K^0\)
average,
\[
  \sin(2\beta_{\rm eff})_{\phi K^0}=0.74\pm0.12,\qquad
  C_{\phi K^0}=-0.09\pm0.12.
\]
HFLAV uses \(S=-\eta_{CP}\sin(2\beta_{\rm eff})\) and quotes
\(\eta_{CP}=-1\) for \(\phi K_S\), so this \(\sin(2\beta_{\rm eff})\) is the
usual \(S_{\phi K_S}\) coefficient in this convention.  The comparison
benchmark on the same HFLAV page is the all-charmonium
\[
  \sin(2\beta)=0.710\pm0.011,
\]
with the strict \(J/\psi K_S\) mode average \(0.712\pm0.011\).  The local
snapshot is \texttt{references/B033/hflav\_triangle\_summer2025\_phiK0.txt},
accessed on 2026-05-16.
sections.relevance:
In anarchic warped models, KK gluons, electroweak KK modes, or flavor-changing
\(Z/H\)-like effects can add new weak phases to \(b\to s\bar s s\)
\(\Delta B=1\) amplitudes.  \(B^0\to\phi K_S^0\) is therefore a diagnostic of
new penguin phases that would not be captured by a pure \(\Delta m_{B_s}\) or
\(\Delta m_{B_d}\) magnitude constraint.  A large departure of
\(S_{\phi K_S}\) from \(S_{\psi K_S}\) would point to decay-amplitude new
physics rather than just the CKM phase measured in the golden charmonium mode.
sections.post_2008:
Since the arXiv:0804.1954 RS-flavor baseline, the old B-factory tension in
penguin CP phases has been replaced by a stable world average compatible with
\(S_{\psi K_S}\).  HFLAV Summer 2025 combines BaBar's 2012 Dalitz analysis,
Belle's 2010 time-dependent Dalitz analysis, and Belle II's 2023
time-dependent \(B^0\to\phi K_S^0\) measurement.  Belle II is the important
new post-B-factory input: the \texttt{belleii\_2023\_phik0} snapshot records
\(S=0.54\pm0.26^{+0.06}_{-0.08}\) and, with HFLAV's \(C=-A\) convention,
\(C=-0.31\pm0.20\pm0.05\).  The present HFLAV average differs from the
charmonium benchmark by only \(0.03\), far below the current \(\phi K^0\)
uncertainty; this difference is recorded explicitly in \texttt{B033.yaml}.
sections.validity:
This is a useful but theory-limited nonleptonic penguin observable.  It is
cleaner as a null test than as a standalone hard bound: the SM prediction is
close to \(\sin(2\beta)\), but subleading amplitudes and hadronic strong
phases are mode dependent.  A production implementation would need a
\(\Delta B=1\) operator basis, RG evolution, and a hadronic amplitude or
phenomenological likelihood.  Treat it as charmless-penguin contextual input
unless the PI chooses a specific hadronic framework.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
\textbf{NO.}  Focused greps over \texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/},
\texttt{flavorConstraints/}, \texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/},
\texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/}, \texttt{scanParams/}, and
\texttt{tests/} found no implementation of \(B\to\phi K\),
\(b\to s\bar s s\) penguin CP asymmetries, \(\sin(2\beta_{\rm eff})\), or
time-dependent charmless \(B\) CP observables.  The nearest adjacent code is
neutral-meson mixing: \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:209} defines the
\(\Delta F=2\) input registry, \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:903}
evaluates \(B_d\) mixing, and \texttt{quarkConstraints/deltaf2.py:922}
evaluates \(B_s\) mixing.  Those do not compute \(\Delta B=1\) penguin decay
amplitudes.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
\textbf{HIGH.}  The existing \(\Delta F=2\) SLL/SLR/VLL/VRR/LR1/LR2 machinery is not
enough.  A live constraint would require \(\Delta B=1\) four-quark penguin
operators, matching from RS sources, QCD running to the \(b\) scale, and a
hadronic treatment of the \(B\to\phi K\) amplitude and strong phases.
sections.key_references:
Process-local raw reference keys before bibliography consolidation:
\texttt{hflav\_triangle\_summer2025\_phiK0},
\texttt{babar\_2012\_b0\_kkks},
\texttt{belle\_2010\_b0\_kkks},
\texttt{belleii\_2023\_phik0}, and
\texttt{csaki\_falkowski\_weiler\_2008}.
